content: The thick outer shell of bone is {{c1::cortical bone}}.
Trabecular bone is located {{c1::centrally}} and contains {{c2::marrow elements}}.
In rheumatoid arthritis, the first erosions arise at the {{c1::bare area just inside the synovium}}.
Cortical bone contains {{c1::tightly packed osteons}} with a central {{c2::Haversian canal}}.
The central Haversian canal of a cortical osteon contains {{c1::neurovascular elements}}.
Trabecular bone consists of osteons loosely arranged in {{c1::struts}} and lacks a central {{c2::Haversian canal}}.
